2604.05969.title: A Formal Security Framework for MCP-Based AI Agents: Threat Taxonomy, Verification Models, and Defense Mechanisms
2604.05969.paper_summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP), introduced by Anthropic in November 2024 and now governed by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation, has rapidly become the de facto standard for connecting large language model (LLM)-based agents to external tools and data sources, with over 97 million monthly SDK downloads and more than 177000 registered tools. However, this explosive adoption has exposed a critical gap: the absence of a unified, formal security framework capable of systematically characterizing, analyzing, and mitigating the diverse threats facing MCP-based agent ecosystems. Existing security research remains fragmented across individual attack papers, isolated benchmarks, and point defense mechanisms. This paper presents MCPSHIELD, a comprehensive formal security framework for MCP-based AI agents. We make four principal contributions: (1) a hierarchical threat taxonomy comprising 7 threat categories and 23 distinct attack vectors organized across four attack surfaces, grounded in the analysis of over 177000 MCP tools; (2) a formal verification model based on labeled transition systems with trust boundary annotations that enables static and runtime analysis of MCP tool interaction chains; (3) a systematic comparative evaluation of 12 existing defense mechanisms, identifying coverage gaps across our threat taxonomy; and (4) a defense in depth reference architecture integrating capability based access control, cryptographic tool attestation, information flow tracking, and runtime policy enforcement. Our analysis reveals that no existing single defense covers more than 34 percent of the identified threat landscape, whereas MCPSHIELD's integrated architecture achieves theoretical coverage of 91 percent. We further identify seven open research challenges that must be addressed to secure the next generation of agentic AI systems.
2604.05969.paper_summary_zh: 模型上下文協議（MCP）於2024年11月由Anthropic推出，現在由Linux基金會的Agentic AI基金會管理，迅速成為將基於大型語言模型（LLM）的代理連接到外部工具和數據源的事實標準，每月SDK下載量超過9700萬，註冊工具超過177000個。<lb/>然而，這一爆炸性的採用暴露了一個關鍵的缺口：缺乏一個統一的、正式的安全框架，能夠系統性地表徵、分析和減輕面臨MCP基礎代理生態系統的多樣威脅。<lb/>現有的安全研究仍然分散在個別攻擊論文、孤立的基準測試和點防禦機制中。<lb/>本文提出了MCPSHIELD，一個針對MCP基礎AI代理的綜合正式安全框架。<lb/>我們做出了四個主要貢獻：（1）一個包含7個威脅類別和23個獨特攻擊向量的分層威脅分類法，這些向量根據對超過177000個MCP工具的分析進行組織；（2）一個基於標記轉換系統的正式驗證模型，帶有信任邊界註釋，能夠靜態和運行時分析MCP工具交互鏈；（3）對12種現有防禦機制的系統比較評估，識別我們的威脅分類法中的覆蓋缺口；以及（4）一個深度防禦參考架構，整合了基於能力的訪問控制、加密工具驗證、信息流追蹤和運行時策略執行。<lb/>我們的分析顯示，現有的單一防禦措施沒有一種能覆蓋超過34%的識別威脅範圍，而MCPSHIELD的集成架構實現了91%的理論覆蓋率。<lb/>我們進一步識別出七個必須解決的開放研究挑戰，以確保下一代代理AI系統的安全。
2604.05969.author: Nirajan Acharya et.al.
